title: Add agent/observability.py â€” silent-failure ledger backed by SQLite
current_state: Pi has at least 166 swallowed-exception sites (passive scan #11). The audit flagged 4 highest-risk: pi_agent.py:336 (_log_worker), pi_agent.py:436 (append_turn), tools_memory.py:514-516 (L3 conflict mark), tools_memory.py:932,953 (_bump_access_sync). Each is a 'except Exception: pass' that loses data with zero signal. No way to know how often these fire or whether the failure rate is climbing.
structural_problem: Silent failures are invisible until they cause downstream symptoms (missing turns in audit, contradictory facts in memory, wrong access counts). By the time the symptom is noticed, the cause is hard to attribute. There is no shared mechanism to record-and-forget.
target_state: One module exporting track_silent(category, exc, context). Records to data/silent_failures.db (SQLite, capped 10k rows, ring evict oldest). Aggregator function recent_failures(hours) returns {category: count} for the /passive scan and the new system_introspect output.
files_affected: tools/tools_project.py (extend system_introspect to include silent_failures_24h in output) â€” minimal change
audit_refs: Passive scan tech debt #11 (166 swallowed exceptions)
design_notes: Schema: silent_failures(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, timestamp TEXT NOT NULL, category TEXT NOT NULL, exception_type TEXT NOT NULL, redacted_message TEXT, context_json TEXT)
API: track_silent(category: str, exc: Exception | None = None, *, context: dict | None = None) -> None
API: cleanup_old(max_rows: int = 10000) -> int (returns rows deleted)
track_silent must NOT raise â€” if SQLite write fails, swallow that too (would defeat the module's purpose to recursively fail)
Use safe_error(exc, audience='public_log') for redacted_message â€” no leaks into the failure log
Lazy DB connect: open on first track_silent call, cache the connection (with self._db_lock)
Auto-cleanup: every Nth call (e.g. every 100), check row count and evict if > 10k
Thread safety: use a threading.Lock around connection access; cleanup_old must use a separate connection
success_criteria: track_silent never raises even if data/silent_failures.db is read-only / disk full (caught internally)
redacted_message field contains no raw paths or keys (uses safe_error)
risk_notes: Recursive failure: if SQLite itself starts failing (disk full), track_silent calls within track_silent would loop. Mitigation: top-level try/except that re-raises only KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit; all other Exception silently swallowed at the boundary.
migration_plan: 1. Create agent/observability.py with track_silent + recent_failures + cleanup_old + DB schema bootstrap
2. Module-level lazy init: _db_connection = None, opened on first track_silent call
3. Add testing/test_observability.py covering all API methods + recursive-failure guard + thread safety
4. Extend tools/tools_project.py:_handle_system_introspect to call recent_failures(24) and include in output dict â€” single new line
7. No wiring into the 4 audit sites yet â€” that is T-104. This ticket is module + infra only.
test_plan: test_track_silent_records â€” insert one, query directly, assert row exists
test_track_silent_never_raises â€” patch sqlite3 to raise on connect; track_silent must return None without raising
test_recent_failures_aggregates â€” insert 5 rows across 3 categories, recent_failures returns correct counts
test_recent_failures_time_window â€” insert old + recent rows, only recent counted
test_eviction_at_cap â€” insert 10005 rows, count after cleanup <= 10000
test_redaction_applied â€” track_silent with exception containing path â†’ redacted_message has no path
test_thread_safety â€” 10 concurrent threads call track_silent, no exceptions, all rows present
test_context_json_roundtrip â€” context={'k':'v'} stored and retrievable
test_system_introspect_includes_silent_failures â€” call introspect, assert key present
